executive_summary: Unit economics are fundamentally broken for autonomous AI medical diagnosis DTC — LTV/CAC ratio of only 1.06x (LTV $338 vs CAC $320) with 7.5% monthly healthcare SaaS churn destroying lifetime value, all three sensitivity scenarios fail, the closest precedent (Babylon Health) collapsed from a $4B valuation, and regulatory prohibition of autonomous diagnosis eliminates pricing power entirely.
score_reasoning:
Score: 20/100
- LTV/CAC Ratio: 3/25 (LTV $338 / CAC $320 = 1.06x; ARPU $39/mo from competitive DTC pricing mid-range, 7.5% monthly churn from wearefounders.uk 2026 healthcare SaaS benchmark, 65% gross margin adjusted for AI compute + compliance, CAC $320 from First Page Sage Medtech consumer benchmark — ratio barely above 1.0x, tier 0-6 bottom third)
- Revenue Model Validation: 7/25 (2 companies using subscription for similar DTC health AI: K Health $270M+ funded and Ada Health $200M+ funded, but BOTH operate with physician oversight and neither does autonomous diagnosis; Babylon Health, the closest autonomous AI precedent, collapsed from $4B; 0 successful precedents for the exact model proposed — tier 0-6 top, entering tier 7-12 bottom)
- Payback Period: 7/25 (12.62 months; CAC $320 / monthly margin $25.35; falls in 7-12 month tier bottom — but misleading because LTV/CAC of 1.06x means the business never truly 'pays back' in a sustainable sense)
- Pricing Power: 3/25 (Ada Health free tier creates strong floor, K Health $29-49/mo and Ada Premium $9.99/mo create narrow paid band <2x spread, 3+ free alternatives exist including Ada free tier and ChatGPT symptom checking, no premium player exists in autonomous DTC diagnosis because it's legally prohibited, zero pricing power — tier 0-6 bottom third)
Total: 3 + 7 + 7 + 3 = 20

data.model_justification: Subscription is the only viable DTC revenue model for ongoing health access, matching K Health ($29-49/mo) and Ada Health ($9.99/mo premium). However, ALL successful healthcare AI diagnostics companies (Aidoc, Viz.ai, Tempus, HeartFlow, PathAI) use B2B enterprise/usage models selling to hospitals — none sell autonomous diagnosis directly to patients. The DTC health AI subscription model has a catastrophic failure record: Babylon Health collapsed from $4B valuation pursuing exactly this approach.

pricing_suggestion.justification: Mid-range of consumer DTC health AI pricing (Ada $9.99, K Health $29-49). Pain intensity is high (from Problem dept), justifying mid-to-high positioning. However, the competitive high end ($500/study) is enterprise B2B pricing inaccessible to a DTC model. Regulatory prohibition of autonomous diagnosis for serious conditions eliminates any premium pricing justification.

unit_economics.churn_source: Healthcare SaaS monthly churn 7.5% (wearefounders.uk 2026 SaaS Benchmarks, updated Feb 2026). Note: 67% increase from 2024 to 2025, reflecting budget pressures and industry consolidation. DTC consumer health apps likely experience even higher churn.
unit_economics.margin_source: SaaS median gross margin 79% (Pavilion 2024 B2B SaaS Benchmarks Report), adjusted down to 65% for healthcare AI due to: (1) heavy AI inference compute costs for medical imaging/diagnostics, (2) regulatory compliance overhead (HIPAA, FDA, GDPR), (3) clinical validation requirements, (4) data storage and security costs for medical data.
unit_economics.cac_source: Medtech Consumer CAC $320 (First Page Sage, August 2025, 12-year dataset). Blended organic+paid. Healthcare SaaS CAC range $600-900 per wearefounders.uk, but that's B2B — consumer DTC is lower. $320 is conservative for a health app requiring regulatory-compliant marketing.

cac_plus_20.assessment: CAC increase to $384 pushes LTV/CAC below 1.0x — acquiring customers would destroy value, making growth actively harmful to the business.

churn_plus_20.assessment: Churn increase to 9.0% monthly reduces LTV to $281.67, pushing LTV/CAC below 1.0x — the already razor-thin margin evaporates entirely with modest retention degradation.

price_minus_20.assessment: Price reduction to $31.20/mo (closer to K Health's lower tier) makes unit economics clearly negative — LTV drops to $270.40 vs CAC $320, meaning every customer acquired is a net loss.

model_precedents.segment: DTC primary care with AI + physician oversight
model_precedents.evidence: $270M+ raised, 24/7 AI-powered primary care, $29-49/mo pricing, but operates WITH licensed physicians — NOT autonomous diagnosis. US-only.
$200M+ raised, #1 medical app in 130+ countries, 6M+ users. Free tier + $9.99/mo premium. NOT a diagnostic tool — triage/guidance only.

model_precedents.segment: DTC AI-powered primary care and diagnosis
model_precedents.evidence: Collapsed 2023 after $4B valuation. Raised $850M+. Closest precedent to this idea — catastrophic failure due to overpromised AI, unsustainable unit economics, regulatory collisions.

model_precedents.segment: B2B2C digital MSK care with AI + clinicians
model_precedents.evidence: IPO May 2025, $853M+ raised, $732M projected 2026 revenue. Successful but: treats MSK not serious conditions, uses AI + human clinicians, sells B2B2C not pure DTC.
Shut down 2025 after 7 years. Hosting+AI costs ~$70/doctor, customers pay <$100/mo. Demonstrates healthcare AI unit economics structurally broken at low price points.

assumptions: ARPU $39/mo based on mid-range of consumer DTC health AI pricing (Ada $9.99, K Health $29-49); pain intensity 'high' from Problem dept justifies mid-to-high positioning
Monthly churn 7.5% from wearefounders.uk Healthcare SaaS benchmark (2026); this may underestimate DTC consumer churn which is typically higher
Gross margin 65% — reduced from SaaS median 79% (Pavilion 2024) due to AI inference costs, regulatory compliance, medical data security, and clinical validation overhead
CAC $320 from First Page Sage Medtech Consumer benchmark (Aug 2025); may underestimate actual CAC given regulatory constraints on healthcare marketing claims
Subscription model assumed because all DTC health AI competitors use subscription/freemium; however no precedent exists for autonomous diagnosis DTC subscription
No freemium conversion adjustment applied — if freemium model used (like Ada), effective ARPU would drop significantly (typical 2-5% conversion), making economics far worse

evidence.quote: Healthcare SaaS platforms face significant retention challenges with a 7.5% monthly churn rate. The sector experienced a 67% spike in revenue churn from 2024 to 2025.
Medtech Consumer CAC: $320, SMB: $921, Middle Market: $4,326, Enterprise: $11,021
Gross Margin on software subscriptions was 79% at median in 2023, which is the same as 2022. The entry level to the top quartile increased to 85% in 2023.
